fields.description:
# Stage-1 BM25F candidate generation over digests + graph-exclusion filter

## Why
Stage 1 narrows a few hundred tickets to ~20 candidates cheaply and deterministically, no LLM/embeddings, and must NOT surface a ticket's own already-related work (parents/children/siblings/linked) — the top source of "related-but-distinct" false flags. DERISKED (epic experiment comment): digest-BM25F recall@20 = 1.0 on 8 paraphrase pairs, STABLE 60→200 corpus; single-query latency 0.69 ms at 800 digests (~14× under budget) — so brute-force is correct and no ANN/vector-DB is needed.

## What
- Brute-force BM25F "More Like This" over `ticket_digest` records (S2). Bespoke ~40 LOC: `rank-bm25` provides only UNWEIGHTED BM25, but we need per-field weighting; at n<1000 bespoke is trivial and avoids a dependency (the recall+latency spikes validate this choice). Field weights (defaults): `problem_keywords` 3.0, `component_or_area` 3.0, `key_entities` 1.0, `propositions` 1.0. Tokenize lowercased on non-alphanumeric, drop stopwords + corpus-boilerplate via `max_doc_freq`. Returns a typed `OverlapCandidate` `[{ticket_id, score, matched_terms}]` (the interface S7 consumes — stated so S7 isn't blind to it). Query side = the query ticket's freshly-extracted digest, passed by the caller.
- **Graph exclusion** BEFORE scoring: new helper `src/rebar/llm/overlap/graph.py::related_ticket_ids(ticket_id, tracker) -> set[str]` that loads states ONCE via `_load_all_states(tracker)` (the same helper `descendants.py` uses) and derives all four relation sets from that one dict: descendants (BFS on `parent_id`, or by calling `descendants.list_descendants(ticket_id, tracker)` and flattening its type-bucketed id lists — NOT by sharing its internal snapshot, which it does not expose), ancestors (walk `parent_id` up), siblings (same `parent_id`; a root ticket with no parent has NO siblings — handled explicitly), and linked tickets (from reduced `deps`). It does NOT refactor `next_batch.py`. At n<1000 one state load is sub-ms (latency spike).
- Cold/stale: corpus tickets whose digest is absent or stale (S2 freshness: content-hash/model/version mismatch — not an age threshold) are SKIPPED; the skip-count is logged so a cold run is distinguishable from a healthy one.
- Overlap floor: a `min_should_match` fraction (default 0.15 of query terms) below which a candidate is not returned; `retrieve()` returns `[]` when nothing clears the floor.
- Errors: any graph-read/scoring exception is caught, logged, returns `[]` (never blocks review_plan).

## Config (LLMConfig, not VerifyConfig)
`overlap_k` (20), `overlap_field_weights` (the 3/3/1/1 map), `overlap_max_doc_freq`, `overlap_min_should_match` (0.15) — all on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_*`); tunables, not core verify config.

## Dependencies
S1 (`ticket_digest` schema/fields), S2 (digest store + freshness). Digest fields are DEFINED in S1.

## Scope
- New `src/rebar/llm/overlap/retrieve.py` (BM25F + `OverlapCandidate`) and `src/rebar/llm/overlap/graph.py` (`related_ticket_ids`); config keys on `LLMConfig`.
- OUT: judge (S6), wiring (S7). Interface for S7: `retrieve(query_digest, corpus, exclude:set[str]) -> list[OverlapCandidate]`.

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_overlap_retrieve.py`: `test_topk` (order); `test_perf` (<10 ms at 300 digests); `test_graph_exclusion` (parents/children/siblings/linked dropped; root-ticket-no-siblings edge); `test_cold_skip` (absent/stale skipped + count logged); `test_boilerplate` (shared boilerplate ≠ match); `test_floor_empty` (nothing clears min_should_match → `[]`); `test_error_empty` (graph error → `[]`, no raise); `test_config` (overlap_k/weights/max_doc_freq/min_should_match read from LLMConfig).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns top-K (default 20) `OverlapCandidate`s by field-weighted BM25F, <10 ms at ~300 digests; `pytest ...::{test_topk,test_perf}`.
- [ ] Candidates EXCLUDE the query's parents/children/siblings/linked via `related_ticket_ids(ticket_id, tracker)` (reusing `descendants.list_descendants`); root-with-no-parent has no siblings; `::test_graph_exclusion`.
- [ ] Absent/stale digests skipped, skip-count logged; `::test_cold_skip`. Boilerplate doesn't drive matches; `::test_boilerplate`.
- [ ] `min_should_match` floor returns `[]` when unmet; a graph/scoring error returns `[]` (never blocks); `::{test_floor_empty,test_error_empty}`.
- [ ] `overlap_k`, field weights, `max_doc_freq`, `min_should_match` configurable on `LLMConfig`; `::test_config`.
